title: Japchae Sweet Potato Glass Noodles

seo.title: How to Make Japchae Sweet Potato Glass Noodles — Easy Korean Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Japchae is a vibrant and flavorful Korean dish featuring stir-fried sweet potato glass noodles with various vegetables and often meat. Learn how to make authentic Japchae with this easy recipe, perfect for a satisfying meal or side dish.

authentic Korean Japchae Sweet Potato Glass Noodles

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Korean Japchae Sweet Potato Glass Noodles recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Japchae Sweet Potato Glass Noodles — a beloved Korean classic

description: Japchae is a beloved Korean classic, a vibrant and savory dish of stir-fried sweet potato glass noodles mixed with an array of colorful vegetables and often beef. Its unique chewy texture and balanced sweet and savory flavors make it a delightful and satisfying meal, perfect as a main course or a festive side dish.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 30 minutes, then drained

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Begin by soaking the sweet potato glass noodles in warm water for about 30 minutes until pliable. Drain thoroughly. Prepare the beef marinade by mixing 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp sesame oil, 1/2 tsp minced garlic, and a pinch of black pepper. Marinate the sliced beef for at least 15 minutes. Prepare the vegetables: julienne the carrot and bell pepper, thinly slice the onion and mushrooms, and blanch the spinach, squeezing out excess water.

instructions.title: Cook the Noodles
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of water to a boil. Cook the soaked and drained glass noodles for 6-8 minutes, or until tender and translucent but still chewy. Drain the noodles and rinse briefly with cold water to prevent sticking. Toss with 1 tsp sesame oil to keep them from clumping together.

instructions.title: Stir-fry Vegetables
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp cooking oil in a large wok or skillet over medium-high heat. Stir-fry each vegetable separately: first the carrots for 1-2 minutes, then the onions for 2-3 minutes, followed by the bell pepper and mushrooms for 2-3 minutes each. Season each batch lightly with a pinch of salt and pepper. Set all cooked vegetables aside.

instructions.title: Cook the Beef
instructions.text: In the same wok, add a little more cooking oil if needed. Stir-fry the marinated beef until fully cooked and lightly browned, about 3-4 minutes. Set the cooked beef aside with the vegetables.

instructions.title: Combine and Sauce
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the cooked noodles, all the stir-fried vegetables, and the cooked beef. Prepare the Japchae sauce by mixing the remaining 3 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp sugar, 2 tbsp sesame oil, and the remaining minced garlic. Pour the sauce over the ingredients in the bowl.

instructions.title: Mix and Serve
instructions.text: Using your hands (wearing gloves is recommended) or tongs, gently but thoroughly mix all the ingredients together, ensuring the sauce is evenly distributed and coats every strand of noodle and vegetable. Taste and adjust seasoning if necessary. Garnish with toasted sesame seeds and serve warm or at room temperature.

tips: Blanch vegetables briefly to retain their crispness and vibrant color before stir-frying, ensuring they dont become soggy when mixed with the noodles.
For a deeper flavor, use rehydrated dried shiitake mushrooms and reserve some of their soaking liquid to add to the sauce.
Japchae is best served warm or at room temperature. If making ahead, add a splash of sesame oil and stir-fry briefly to refresh the noodles before serving.
article: Japchae Sweet Potato Glass Noodles is a beloved Korean Pasta and Noodles dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
